title: Innovative Nutty Chinese Pork Hot Pot
description: A comforting and crispy Chinese dish featuring gelatine leaves and rest-meat, served in a copper pot. This macrobiotic-friendly hot pot is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Slice the rested pork shoulder into thin strips and set aside.
Soak the gelatine leaves in cold water for 5 minutes, then drain and cut into small pieces.
In a copper pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat and saute the sliced ginger until fragrant.
Add the pork strips and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, peanuts, and star anise, and stir to combine.
Add the gelatine pieces and stir to dissolve.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the chopped scallions and serve hot.
Serve the hot pot in the copper pot, allowing each guest to cook their own ingredients in the simmering broth.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
